title: westringia eremicola
intro: Westringia eremicola, also known as the coastal rosemary or emu bush, is a species of flowering plant native to Western Australia. This shrub has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Westringia eremicola include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and support immune function. Westringia eremicola can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
